tasks.instruction: Przeczytaj ponizszy tekst. Zdecyduj, ktore zdania (1.1-1.8) sa zgodne z trescia tekstu (T – True), ktore sa niezgodne (F – False), a ktore zawieraja informacje niepodane w tekscie (NI – No Information).
tasks.text:
WILLIAM SHAKESPEARE

William Shakespeare is widely regarded as the greatest writer in the English language. He was born in 1564 in Stratford-upon-Avon, a small town in central England. His father, John Shakespeare, was a successful glove maker and local politician. William attended the local grammar school, where he studied Latin and classical literature, but he never went to university.

At the age of 18, Shakespeare married Anne Hathaway, who was eight years older than him. Together they had three children: Susanna, and twins named Hamnet and Judith. Sometime in the late 1580s, Shakespeare left Stratford and moved to London, where he began his career in the theatre as both an actor and a playwright.

In London, Shakespeare joined a theatre company called the Lord Chamberlain's Men, which later became known as the King's Men. The company performed at the famous Globe Theatre, a round, open-air theatre on the south bank of the River Thames. The Globe could hold up to 3,000 spectators. The cheapest tickets cost just one penny, and those audience members had to stand in front of the stage. They were called "groundlings."

During his career, Shakespeare wrote approximately 37 plays and 154 sonnets. His plays are usually divided into three categories: comedies, tragedies, and histories. Some of his most famous tragedies include Hamlet, Macbeth, Othello, and Romeo and Juliet. His comedies, such as A Midsummer Night's Dream and Much Ado About Nothing, are still performed regularly around the world today.

Shakespeare retired to Stratford-upon-Avon around 1613 and died there on 23 April 1616, at the age of 52. His works have been translated into every major language and continue to be studied and performed more than 400 years after his death. Many common English phrases, such as "break the ice" and "heart of gold," were first used by Shakespeare.

items.statement: Shakespeare's father worked as a glove maker.
Shakespeare studied at Oxford University before becoming a writer.
Shakespeare and his wife had three children.
The Globe Theatre could hold up to 5,000 people.
Shakespeare earned more money from his sonnets than from his plays.
Romeo and Juliet is classified as one of Shakespeare's tragedies.
Shakespeare's plays were first published as a complete collection during his lifetime.
